ALL SPECIALTIES / DENTAL ASSISTANT / VIRGINIA

Photo: Unsplash / Unsplash

Virginia Dental Assistant Registration

Virginia Dental Assistant license requirements, fees, and steps.

Last updated 2026-03-22 · Sources verified

  • New Applicant
  • Reciprocity
  • Renewal

New Applicant

Dental Assistant applications in Virginia run through Virginia Board of Dentistry. Use the checklist below before starting your application.

Required documents

  • Proof of Training - Certificate from CODA-accredited program or documentation of on-the-job training hours.
  • DANB Certification - Official CDA certification from the Dental Assisting National Board, if applicable.
  • Radiography Certification - Proof of radiography competency/certification required for taking x-rays.

Key timeline factors

  • Board processing 4 to 6 Weeks
  • Fingerprinting Not required for most applicants.
  • Application completeness Missing or mismatched documents are the most common reason timelines extend.

Reciprocity

Virginia does not require state licensure for general dental assistants. DANB national certification is recognized and recommended.

  1. STEP 1

    Meet Eligibility Requirements

    Ensure you meet the qualifications for Expanded Duties Dental Assistant (EDDA) registration, which typically includes graduation from a CODA-accredited dental assisting program OR completion of a board-approved expanded duties course AND certification by DANB OR 2 years of full-time dental assisting experience, plus current Basic Life Support (BLS) or CPR certification.

    Fee: $0 · Timeline: 4 to 6 Weeks

  2. STEP 2

    Register an Account on DHP Online Services

    Navigate to the DHP Online Services portal at https://www.license.dhp.virginia.gov/apply/. Click 'Register a Person' to create a new user account with a unique username and password. This account will be used to manage your application and future renewals.

    Fee: $0 · Timeline: 4 to 6 Weeks

  3. STEP 3

    Complete and Submit the Online Application

    Log in to your DHP Online Services account. Select 'Apply for a New License/Certification' and choose the 'Expanded Duties Dental Assistant' application. Complete all required sections of the application form and pay the non-refundable application fee online.

    Fee: $0 · Timeline: 4 to 6 Weeks

  4. STEP 4

    Arrange for Background Check and Submit Supporting Documents

    Submit fingerprints for a criminal background check as instructed by DHP after your application submission. Have official transcripts sent directly from your CODA-accredited program to the Virginia Board of Dentistry. Additionally, provide proof of current CPR/BLS certification, and if applicable, proof of DANB certification.

    Fee: $0 · Timeline: 4 to 6 Weeks

  5. STEP 5

    Complete the Virginia Jurisprudence Examination

    Take and pass the Virginia Dental Jurisprudence Examination. This exam is often available online through the DHP portal or linked resources and assesses your knowledge of Virginia's dental laws and regulations.

    Fee: $0 · Timeline: 4 to 6 Weeks

  6. STEP 6

    Receive Your Registration

    The Virginia Board of Dentistry will review your completed application and all supporting documentation. Once approved, your Expanded Duties Dental Assistant registration will be issued and will be viewable on the DHP License Lookup portal.

    Fee: $0 · Timeline: 4 to 6 Weeks

Fees and Costs

Application fee
$0
Background check
Varies by provider
Jurisprudence exam
$0
Total estimated cost
$0

Always confirm current fees on the board site before submitting.

Application page
Open application

Renewal

Dental Assistant renewal in Virginia follows the board cycle below.

Renewal cycle
Every 2 years
Renewal fee
$30
Continuing education
See board

Frequently Asked Questions

Does Virginia require a dental assistant license?

No, Virginia does not require state licensure for general dental assistants. However, expanded functions and radiography may require permits or certifications.

Is DANB certification recognized in Virginia?

Yes, DANB CDA certification is recognized and often preferred by employers in Virginia.

Can I take dental x-rays in Virginia?

Yes, but a radiography permit or certification is required.

How much does it cost to become a dental assistant in Virginia?

There is no state application fee for general dental assistants in Virginia. However, DANB CDA exam costs $450, and radiography certifications may have additional fees.

Renewal
